DOMAIN_SEPARATOR().details: Returns the domain separator used in the encoding of the signature for {permit}, as defined by {EIP712}.

acceptOwnership().details: The new owner accepts the ownership transfer.

allowance(address,address).details: Returns the remaining number of tokens that `spender` will be allowed to spend on behalf of `owner` through {transferFrom}. This is zero by default. This value changes when {approve} or {transferFrom} are called.

balanceOf(address).details: Returns the value of tokens owned by `account`.

cancelOwnershipHandover().details: Cancels the two-step ownership handover to the caller, if any.

completeOwnershipHandover(address).details: Allows the owner to complete the two-step ownership handover to `pendingOwner`. Reverts if there is no existing ownership handover requested by `pendingOwner`.

computeBalance(uint256[],uint256,uint256.details: Similar to V2's `_getTokenBalanceGivenInvariantAndAllOtherBalances` in StableMath. The pool must round up for the Vault to round in the protocol's favor when calling this function.

params.balancesLiveScaled18: Token balances after paying yield fees, applying decimal scaling and rates
params.invariantRatio: The ratio of the new invariant (after an operation) to the old
params.tokenInIndex: The index of the token we're computing the balance for, sorted in token registration order

returns._0: The new balance of the selected token, after the operation

computeInvariant(uint256[],uint8).details: The invariant is: inv = projectBalance * projectTokenRate + reserveBalance. This represents the total value in the pool, in terms of reserve tokens.

decimals().details: Returns the decimals places of the token.

getAggregateFeePercentages().details: These are determined by the current protocol and pool creator fees, set in the `ProtocolFeeController`.

returns.aggregateSwapFeePercentage: The aggregate percentage fee applied to swaps
returns.aggregateYieldFeePercentage: The aggregate percentage fee applied to yield

getCurrentLiveBalances().details: Note that live balances will not necessarily be accurate if the pool is in Recovery Mode. Withdrawals in Recovery Mode do not make external calls (including those necessary for updating live balances), so if there are withdrawals, raw and live balances will be out of sync until Recovery Mode is disabled.

returns.balancesLiveScaled18: Token balances after paying yield fees, applying decimal scaling and rates

returns.data: A struct containing all dynamic LBP parameters
A struct containing all immutable LBP parameters

getHookFlags().details: For each flag set to true, the Vault will call the corresponding hook.

returns.hookFlags: Flags indicating which hooks are supported for LBPs

getMaximumInvariantRatio().details: Since this pool only allows balanced liquidity operations (owner adds before sale, anyone removes after), unbalanced operations should never occur. We return extreme bounds to indicate this.

returns._0: The maximum invariant ratio for a pool during unbalanced add liquidity
The maximum swap fee percentage for a pool
The migration parameters (duration, successor Weighted Pool config)
Address of the migration router

getMinimumInvariantRatio().details: Since this pool only allows balanced liquidity operations (owner adds before sale, anyone removes after), unbalanced operations should never occur. We return extreme bounds to indicate this.

returns._0: The minimum invariant ratio for a pool during unbalanced remove liquidity
The minimum swap fee percentage for a pool

getProjectToken().details: This is the token being distributed through the sale. It is also available in the immutable data, but this getter is provided as a convenience for those who only need the project token address.

returns._0: The address of the project token

getProjectTokenRate().details: This is included in the immutable data, but also added here for convenience.

getRate().details: The meaning of this rate depends on the context. Note that there may be an error associated with a token rate, and the caller might require a certain rounding direction to ensure correctness. This (legacy) interface does not take a rounding direction or return an error, so great care must be taken when interpreting and using rates in downstream computations.

returns._0: The current token rate

getReserveToken().details: This is the token exchanged for the project token (usually a stablecoin or WETH). It is also available in the immutable data, but this getter is provided as a convenience for those who only need the reserve token address.

returns._0: The address of the reserve token
18-decimal FP value of the static swap fee percentage
Index of the project token in the pool
returns._1: Index of the reserve token in the pool

returns.balancesRaw: Current native decimal balances of the pool tokens, sorted in token registration order
returns.lastBalancesLiveScaled18: Last saved live balances, sorted in token registration order
returns.tokenInfo: Token info structs (type, rate provider, yield flag), sorted in token registration order
returns.tokens: Pool tokens, sorted in token registration order
List of tokens in the pool, sorted in registration order

returns._0: Address of the trusted router (i.e., one that reliably reports the sender)

isProjectTokenSwapInBlocked().details: Note that theoretically, anyone holding project tokens could create a new pool alongside the LBP that did allow "selling" project tokens. This restriction only applies to the primary LBP.

returns._0: If true, acquired project tokens cannot be traded for reserve in this pool

isSwapEnabled().details: For LBPs, swaps are enabled during the token sale, between the start and end times. Note that this does not check whether the pool or Vault is paused, which can only happen through governance action. This can be checked using `getPoolConfig` on the Vault, or by calling `getLBPoolDynamicData` here.

returns._0: True if the sale is in progress

name().details: Returns the name of the token.

onAfterAddLiquidity(address,address,uint.details: Called if the `shouldCallAfterAddLiquidity` flag is set in the configuration. The Vault will ignore `hookAdjustedAmountsInRaw` unless `enableHookAdjustedAmounts` is true. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.amountsInRaw: Actual amounts of tokens added, sorted in token registration order
params.amountsInScaled18: Actual amounts of tokens added, sorted in token registration order
params.balancesScaled18: Current pool balances, sorted in token registration order
params.kind: The add liquidity operation type (e.g., proportional, custom)
params.pool: Pool address, used to fetch pool information from the Vault (pool config, tokens, etc.)
params.router: The address (usually a router contract) that initiated an add liquidity operation on the Vault
params.userData: Additional (optional) data provided by the user

returns._0: True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement
returns._1: New amountsInRaw, potentially modified by the hook

onAfterInitialize(uint256[],uint256,byte.details: Called if the `shouldCallAfterInitialize` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.bptAmountOut: Amount of pool tokens minted during initialization
params.userData: Optional, arbitrary data sent with the encoded request

returns._0: True if the pool accepts the initialization results

onAfterRemoveLiquidity(address,address,u.details: Called if the `shouldCallAfterRemoveLiquidity` flag is set in the configuration. The Vault will ignore `hookAdjustedAmountsOutRaw` unless `enableHookAdjustedAmounts` is true. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.amountsOutRaw: Actual amount of tokens to receive, sorted in token registration order
params.amountsOutScaled18: Scaled amount of tokens to receive, sorted in token registration order
params.balancesScaled18: Current pool balances, sorted in token registration order
params.bptAmountIn: Amount of pool tokens to burn
params.kind: The type of remove liquidity operation (e.g., proportional, custom)
params.pool: Pool address, used to fetch pool information from the Vault (pool config, tokens, etc.)
params.router: The address (usually a router contract) that initiated a remove liquidity operation on the Vault
params.userData: Additional (optional) data provided by the user

returns._0: True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement
returns._1: New amountsOutRaw, potentially modified by the hook

onAfterSwap((uint8,address,address,uint2.details: Called if the `shouldCallAfterSwap` flag is set in the configuration. The Vault will ignore `hookAdjustedAmountCalculatedRaw` unless `enableHookAdjustedAmounts` is true. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.params: Swap parameters (see above for struct definition)

returns._0: True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement
returns._1: New amount calculated, potentially modified by the hook

params.router: The router used for the operation

returns._0: success True (allowing the operation to proceed) if the owner is calling through the trusted router

onBeforeInitialize(uint256[],bytes).details: Take care to set the start time far enough in advance to allow for funding; otherwise the pool will remain unfunded and need to be redeployed. Note that initialization does not pass the router address, so we cannot directly check that here, though there has to be a call on the trusted router for its `getSender` to be non-zero.

params.exactAmountsInScaled18: The amounts being used to initialize the pool (18-decimal scaled)

returns._0: success Always true: allow the initialization to proceed if all conditions have been met
success Always true; if removing liquidity is not allowed, revert here with a more specific error

onBeforeSwap((uint8,uint256,uint256[],ui.details: Called if the `shouldCallBeforeSwap` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.params: Swap parameters (see PoolSwapParams for struct definition)
params.pool: Pool address, used to get pool information from the Vault (poolData, token config, etc.)

returns._0: True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement

onComputeDynamicSwapFeePercentage((uint8.details: Called if the `shouldCallComputeDynamicSwapFee` flag is set in the configuration. Hook contracts should use the `onlyVault` modifier to guarantee this is only called by the Vault.

params.params: Swap parameters (see PoolSwapParams for struct definition)
params.pool: Pool address, used to get pool information from the Vault (poolData, token config, etc.)
params.staticSwapFeePercentage: 18-decimal FP value of the static swap fee percentage, for reference

returns._0: True if the pool wishes to proceed with settlement
returns._1: Value of the swap fee percentage, as an 18-decimal FP value

onRegister(address,address,(address,uint.details: Returns true if registration was successful; false will revert with `HookRegistrationFailed`.

params.pool: Address of the pool (must be this contract for LBPs: the pool is also the hook)
params.tokenConfig: The token configuration of the pool being registered (e.g., type)

returns._0: success True if the hook allowed the registration, false otherwise

params.params: Swap parameters (see above for struct definition)

returns.amountCalculatedScaled18: Calculated amount for the swap operation

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

ownershipHandoverExpiresAt(address).details: Returns the expiry timestamp for the two-step ownership handover to `pendingOwner`.

pendingOwner().details: Returns the address of the pending owner.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

requestOwnershipHandover().details: Request a two-step ownership handover to the caller. The request will automatically expire in 48 hours (172800 seconds) by default.

symbol().details: Returns the symbol of the token.

totalSupply().details: Returns the value of tokens in existence.

transferOwnership(address).details: Starts the ownership transfer of the contract to a new account. Replaces the pending transfer if there is one. Can only be called by the current owner. Setting `newOwner` to the zero address is allowed; this can be used to cancel an initiated ownership transfer.

returns._0: version The stored contract version

computeBalance(uint256[],uint256,uint256.notice: Computes a new token balance, given the invariant growth ratio and all other balances.

getAggregateFeePercentages().notice: Gets the aggregate swap and yield fee percentages for a pool.

getCurrentLiveBalances().notice: Gets the current live balances of the pool as fixed point, 18-decimal numbers.

getFixedPriceLBPoolDynamicData().notice: Get dynamic pool data relevant to swap/add/remove calculations.

getFixedPriceLBPoolImmutableData().notice: Get immutable pool data relevant to swap/add/remove calculations.

getHookFlags().notice: Return the HookFlags struct, which indicates which hooks this contract supports.

getMigrationParameters().notice: Retrieve the migration parameters for an LBP.

getMigrationRouter().notice: Returns the migration router used to migrate the liquidity.

getProjectToken().notice: Get the project token for this LBP.

getProjectTokenRate().notice: Get the project token rate (price) in terms of reserve tokens.

getRate().notice: An 18 decimal fixed point number representing the exchange rate of one token to another related token.

getReserveToken().notice: Get the reserve token for this LBP.

getStaticSwapFeePercentage().notice: Fetches the static swap fee percentage for the pool.

getTokenIndices().notice: Convenience function to return the token indices (determined by the addresses).

getTokenInfo().notice: Gets the raw data for the pool: tokens, token info, raw balances, and last live balances.

getTokens().notice: Gets the tokens registered in the pool.

getTrustedRouter().notice: Returns the trusted router, which is used to initialize and seed the pool.

isProjectTokenSwapInBlocked().notice: Indicate whether project tokens can be sold back into the pool.

isSwapEnabled().notice: Indicate whether or not swaps are enabled for this pool.

onAfterAddLiquidity(address,address,uint.notice: Hook to be executed after adding liquidity.

onAfterInitialize(uint256[],uint256,byte.notice: Hook to be executed after pool initialization.

onAfterRemoveLiquidity(address,address,u.notice: Hook to be executed after removing liquidity.

onAfterSwap((uint8,address,address,uint2.notice: Called after a swap to perform further actions once the balances have been updated by the swap.

onBeforeAddLiquidity(address,address,uin.notice: Allow the owner to add liquidity before the start of the sale.

onBeforeInitialize(uint256[],bytes).notice: Block initialization if the sale has already started, verify sender, and validate amounts.

onBeforeRemoveLiquidity(address,address,.notice: Only remove liquidity before the sale (to correct mistakes) or after the sale (withdrawal of proceeds).

onBeforeSwap((uint8,uint256,uint256[],ui.notice: Called before a swap to give the Pool an opportunity to perform actions.

onComputeDynamicSwapFeePercentage((uint8.notice: Called after `onBeforeSwap` and before the main swap operation, if the pool has dynamic fees.

onRegister(address,address,(address,uint.notice: Hook to be executed when the pool is registered.

onSwap((uint8,uint256,uint256[],uint256,.notice: Execute a swap in the pool.
